cards.title: How Perfectionism Undermines Creativity in Children
cards.body: Perfectionism in children increases anxiety and reduces creative thinking. A strong self-concept and healthy risk-taking foster imagination and innovation instead.

cards.title: Why Autistic Emerging Adults May Struggle to Launch
cards.body: Autistic adults face barriers like skills gaps, capacity limits, or readiness issues—not motivation problems. Understanding which specific barrier blocks progress helps parents provide better support.

cards.title: Old Enough to Know
cards.body: Children bring natural questions about sex and death to conversations and therapy. Parents can help them make sense of these innate curiosities in healthy ways.

cards.title: Are You Raising a Boy Who Doesn't Like Sports?
cards.body: Athletic boys have traditionally dominated childhood's social landscape. Parents of non-sporty boys navigate different social pressures and identity development.

cards.title: Playful Presence: Helping Teens With OCD Redirect Attention
cards.body: Mindfulness for teens with OCD can begin with laughter rather than silence. Simple games help them practice attention, flexibility, and the freedom to make mistakes.

cards.title: I Was Worried Kids Would Kill My Creativity
cards.body: Parenting can fuel creativity rather than diminish it, offering unexpected inspiration. A four-year-old's imaginative observations—like kale stalks becoming giraffes—reveal the kaleidoscopic thinking children bring.
